questions.objective_ref: Define Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in plain language and explain what problem it solves.
Describe the three-step RAG pipeline in sequence: retrieve, augment, generate.
Explain why RAG reduces hallucination compared with a foundation model used without retrieval.
Distinguish RAG from fine-tuning — what each method changes and when each is more appropriate.
Give two real-world examples of applications that benefit from RAG and explain why retrieval is the key enabler.
Name the advanced technical components of RAG — embeddings, vector databases, similarity search, chunking — and recognise that these are studied in Week 14.
